title: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Removal (Cont'd)

figures_detected.description: Lower joint tightening bolts on steering gear pinion — shows bolts to slacken
Engine jack supporting transmission housing with engine centre member and rear engine mounting visible
M/T transmission linkage showing control rod and support rod
Steering gear mounting bolts and dust boot retaining strap on pinion
Steering gear being displaced through wheel arch for removal

steps.text: Slacken lower joint tightening bolts and remove lower joint from steering gear pinion. Ensure joint and pinion are marked for correct alignment during re-assembly.
notes: CAUTION: Do not forcibly spread flange of lower joint when disassembling.

steps.text: Support transmission housing with engine jack and remove engine centre member and rear engine mounting (see EM section for details).
Disconnect transmission linkage (support rod and control rod for manual transmission vehicles and transmission cable and mounting bracket for N-CVT vehicles) and place aside.
Slacken dust boot retaining strap and remove dust boot from pinion.

For LHD vehicles, rotate gear and displace through right-hand wheel arch until left-hand tie rod is clear to drop out through bottom of engine bay, then remove assembly. For RHD vehicles, rotate gear and displace through left-hand wheel arch until right-hand tie-rod is clear to drop out through bottom of engine bay, then remove assembly.

content_blocks.text: CAUTION: Do not forcibly spread flange of lower joint when disassembling.

model_notes: Step 7 distinguishes between M/T (support rod and control rod) and N-CVT (transmission cable and mounting bracket) vehicles. Step 10 distinguishes between LHD and RHD vehicles for gear removal direction.
